summary: rc.19 bootstrap consolidation — collapse three drifted end-client bootstrap surfaces (Claude CLAUDE.md / Codex root AGENTS.md / Cursor .cursor/rules) into a single canonical source at .fabric/AGENTS.md (hoisted to packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts so server and CLI both consume from one place). Introduces marker rename fabric:knowledge-base → fabric:bootstrap (one-time migration under fab doctor --fix only), Cursor migration from legacy flat-file .cursor/rules to proper .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc directory rule with YAML front-matter, two-layer byte-level drift detection in fab doctor (L1: canonical ↔ snapshot; L2: snapshot+project-rules ↔ three-end managed blocks) with drift→abort/--fix→overwrite semantics, deletion of 4 orphaned templates + all dead-pointer references to v1.x .fabric/bootstrap/README.md, and self-host of this repo to dogfood the new pipeline.
approach: Three-phase strategy: (1) Foundation — hoist canonical content and marker constants to packages/shared so both CLI install and server doctor consume from one place (resolves cross-package boundary: server has zero dep on cli). (2) Pipeline — fab install writes .fabric/AGENTS.md snapshot from canonical, then propagates to three ends via per-client thin-shell writers (Claude: real @import; Codex: byte-copy managed block; Cursor: byte-copy managed block + YAML front-matter). Marker rename surfaces as separate doctor fixable_error for explicit operator audit trail. fab doctor adds two-layer drift detection with byte-level comparison (NO normalization — CRLF differences trigger drift surfacing install-side bugs). (3) Cleanup + validation — delete 4 orphan templates + scrub dead references; self-host on this repo; rewrite install integration tests for new marker + new paths; add doctor service-layer drift + migration tests with mandatory FABRIC_HOME isolation. Order is critical: TASK-01 (shared canonical) unblocks everything; TASK-02 (snapshot) feeds TASK-03 (propagation); TASK-04 (marker migration) must run before TASK-05 (L1/L2 drift) in doctor dispatcher so post-rename state is what L1/L2 see.

design_decisions.decision: Canonical bootstrap content lives in packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts as a TS const string export
design_decisions.rationale: packages/server has zero workspace dependency on packages/cli; both CLI install (writer) and server doctor (drift comparator) need the same canonical content. Shared is the only common dependency. Mirrors rc.18 banner-i18n shared module pattern.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Content in TS is less human-friendly to edit than a .md file, but the locked草案 is short (~500 chars) and rarely-changing. Single source of truth wins.

design_decisions.decision: Marker rename surfaces as separate fixable_error code (bootstrap_marker_migration_required) with own ledger event (bootstrap_marker_migrated)
design_decisions.rationale: Explicit operator audit trail. Mirrors mcp_config_migrated pattern (doctor.ts:4395). Migration runs FIRST in runDoctorFix dispatcher so subsequent L1/L2 drift inspections see post-rename state.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Extra fixable_error code vs bundling into L2 drift fix. Acceptable; rc.19 brief explicitly calls out the marker rename concern.

design_decisions.rationale: Per locked clarification decision NEW-4. Cleaner fresh installs; users opt in by creating the file; reduces idempotent-or-preserve binary in install.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Less discoverable. Mitigated by doc/scan recommendation surfacing it.

design_decisions.decision: Clean-slate deletion of legacy .cursor/rules flat file when present (no migration shim)
design_decisions.rationale: Memory feedback_clean_slate.md — pre-user phase prefers clean-slate refactors over preservation. .cursor/rules flat-file path is a pre-existing drift bug from real Cursor convention.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Internal dev workflows in this repo may have rc.18 state to clean up. Mitigated: TASK-07 self-host explicitly migrates this repo as part of rc.19 validation.

design_decisions.decision: Byte-level drift detection with NO normalization layer (CRLF differences must trigger drift)
design_decisions.rationale: Per task brief constraint — CRLF normalization would hide install-side bugs. The drift inspector reads raw utf8 and compares directly. Adds an explicit CRLF regression guard test.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Windows CI runners with autocrlf may produce false positives. Mitigated: .gitattributes 'text eol=lf' on canonical artifacts (out of scope for rc.19 but flagged for follow-up if surfaces).

design_decisions.decision: Doctor drift detection duplicates ~40 LOC of managed-block-write logic instead of cross-package importing CLI's writers
design_decisions.rationale: packages/server has zero dep on packages/cli — adding the dep would create a runtime/install-time package boundary blur. Duplication is small and the logic is simple regex+replace.
design_decisions.tradeoff: Two places to maintain. Mitigated: TASK-07 self-host runs both install AND doctor on this repo and asserts byte-equality between their outputs.

design_decisions.decision: Test split — install-side tests in packages/cli/__tests__/integration/install-skills-and-hooks.test.ts + new bootstrap-snapshot.test.ts; doctor-side tests in packages/server/src/services/doctor.test.ts (service layer, NOT CLI-mocked)
design_decisions.rationale: Per exploration test_concerns 'Mocked-server tests don't catch real server-side regressions' (high severity). rc.17 onwards convention: service-layer tests for real logic; CLI mock tests only for flag plumbing (unchanged in rc.19 so skipped).
design_decisions.tradeoff: Tests live in two packages. Acceptable; mirrors codebase architecture.

data_flow.diagram: BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL (shared) → fab install → .fabric/AGENTS.md (L1 snapshot) + .fabric/project-rules.md? (only-if-exists) → three writers → CLAUDE.md @-import + AGENTS.md managed block + .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc managed block. fab doctor inspects: canonical ↔ snapshot (L1) and snapshot+rules ↔ three blocks (L2); --fix re-replays the install writers.

transformations: Codex: BOOTSTRAP_REGEX in-place replace OR append-with-blank-separator
Cursor: YAML front-matter (alwaysApply: true) + managed block; delete legacy flat-file .cursor/rules

stages.input: Files with legacy fabric:knowledge-base markers (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules, .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc)
stages.output: Same files with fabric:bootstrap markers + bootstrap_marker_migrated events in .fabric/events.jsonl

stages.input: BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL + on-disk .fabric/AGENTS.md + on-disk three-end managed blocks
stages.output: DoctorCheck with code='bootstrap_snapshot_drift' (L1) and/or 'managed_block_drift' (L2) when bytes differ; status='ok' otherwise
transformations: BOOTSTRAP_REGEX extraction for managed block bodies

stages.output: Restored .fabric/AGENTS.md (L1 fix) + restored three-end managed blocks (L2 fix)
transformations: BOOTSTRAP_REGEX replace for L2 across three targets

stages.input: This repo's pre-rc.19 state (existing AGENTS.md with legacy fabric:knowledge-base markers)
stages.output: Migrated repo with .fabric/AGENTS.md + updated CLAUDE.md + .cursor/rules/fabric-bootstrap.mdc + new fabric:bootstrap markers everywhere

dependencies: @fenglimg/fabric-shared (BOOTSTRAP_CANONICAL + marker constants — consumed by both cli and server)
Existing event-ledger.ts schema (extended with bootstrap_marker_migrated variant)
Existing doctor.ts inspect/check/fix trio convention (extended with three new check pairs)
